[2018-07-02 17:25] cogito3 (Cogito): Fast Tarsier at 5R would be one of the best attackers in Prismata; Phage is that plus Drone, and even has a bonus red sink ability. It's one of the best units in the game

[2018-07-02 17:33] darkeisbein: simply because 5R for a fast tarsier is pretty good

[2018-07-02 17:35] cactus_wren (cactus): i killed his wild drones, i had more money than him, i had forcefields for the end game
[2018-07-02 17:35] cactus_wren (cactus): my tarsiers came off cooldown first

[2018-07-02 17:38] leary93: being able to defend fake threat by chaining them and then defend real threat once you killed the wilds
[2018-07-02 17:39] leary93: and with the vores he was up on pressure all game

[2018-07-02 17:40] leary93: when Blood pact is around
[2018-07-02 17:40] leary93: and yeah, more wild drones wouldve been really good, since you had to defend way earlier than he had, and econ doesn't matter that much later on

--- Thread (38 messages, 2018-07-03) ---
[2018-07-03 02:30] willflame: what was my mistake in this game? 2VDUn-fWFTF
[2018-07-03 02:30] swapgo: Turn 2 Natural Animus is gross
[2018-07-03 02:30] swapgo: Also Hold on i'll see the game in a sec
[2018-07-03 02:31] willflame: bc we were pretty close in damage but i was ahead in economy, but toward the end i guess i used my drones badly
[2018-07-03 02:31] willflame: should i have clicked Ossified Drone instead of building a Grimbotch?
[2018-07-03 02:34] swapgo: So far both of you don't seem to realize Grimbotch exists
[2018-07-03 02:36] swapgo: I think you durdled too much. You built attack earlier, but Doug got a wall of soak with Xenos which gave them the window to both get an Asteri and use Botches efficiently
[2018-07-03 02:37] willflame: when should i have switched from Tarsiers to Grimbotches?
[2018-07-03 02:37] swapgo: When their Ts started to go online
[2018-07-03 02:37] Deleted User (Deleted User): like grimbotch is a higher value purchase than tarsier by a long shot
[2018-07-03 02:38] Deleted User (Deleted User): so the only consideration to not is when they won't get full value, or it's low inflation enough it's equivalent enough you want extra botch seeds
[2018-07-03 02:38] Deleted User (Deleted User): full value= no absorb denial, they soak for 2 on death
[2018-07-03 02:39] swapgo: Also if Doug made Botches instead of T's when you decided to not wall their Xeno you would've been in deep way faster
[2018-07-03 02:40] supergreyman (Greyman): When both players are at sufficiently high econ for a given set, the first attacker has an initiative and has an upper hand.
[2018-07-03 02:41] willflame: i thought buying a Wall to defend for 1 attack wasn't worth it
[2018-07-03 02:41] swapgo: It isn't but leaving yourself open to double botch there would've been too risky
[2018-07-03 02:41] swapgo: Luckily your opponent didn't saw the line
[2018-07-03 02:42] willflame: couldn't i have defended with a Forcefield?
[2018-07-03 02:42] willflame: i was still ahead in econ
[2018-07-03 02:42] swapgo: Yes but that sets you back, being on Fs, not being able to rack up for Antima or to have B to set up Xeno
[2018-07-03 02:43] supergreyman (Greyman): Being ahead in econ doesn't mean much when he can quickly amass attackers.
[2018-07-03 02:43] supergreyman (Greyman): Once he's able to set up a lot of attackers, your drones would be too busy making defense.
[2018-07-03 02:44] willflame: even with FF i could've made double botch to match his attack, though
[2018-07-03 02:44] willflame: and i had two Tarsiers from previous turn compared to his singular Xeno
[2018-07-03 02:45] willflame: i guess Asteri Cannon is just higher value than i give it

[2018-07-03 15:59] leary93: holding arka is better in some cases and worse in others

[2018-07-03 16:00] leary93: But generally holding arka is better, value wise
[2018-07-03 16:00] leary93: because you'd have to build defense before your opp would have to rebuild 4 defense

[2018-07-05 19:05] cogito3 (Cogito): it's a good idea if inflation is high
[2018-07-05 19:05] cogito3 (Cogito): but my point is, if there's no good red attacker in the random set, you probably didn't want to buy the Animus. (There are exceptions of course, there are always exceptions)
[2018-07-05 19:10] swapgo: You buy the animus if there's no good attacker in the random set at all
[2018-07-05 19:11] swapgo: But yeah, 2 Attack and 2 Soak accross 3 turns isn't terrible but to make that happen across 3 turns you have to work some hard reads and math
[2018-07-05 19:11] swapgo: and even then you just used an expensive Grimbotch
[2018-07-05 19:14] cachotognax: ok I really need to ask, what do you mean by high/low inflation games?
[2018-07-05 19:15] swapgo: It's not high/low inflation games, it's high/low inflation gamestates
[2018-07-05 19:15] swapgo: I don't think I can explain it eloquently though
[2018-07-05 19:15] rezyk: http://107.170.7.25/prismata/math.html
[2018-07-05 19:21] cachotognax: I'll read it later, rn  I'm not capable of holding attention for more than 3 sentences
[2018-07-05 19:27] swapgo: The main takeaway is that having a thing one turn earlier is way more effective than having it one turn later, because of the way econ and attack works
[2018-07-05 19:27] swapgo: Like, if you have 3 Gold and don't buy a drone, you have 3 gold next turn, if you have 3 Gold and buy a Drone, you have 1 Gold and a Drone that's worth 3 Gold
[2018-07-05 19:28] swapgo: Yeah but this is also why only make defense at the last moment
[2018-07-05 19:28] swapgo: Because any money not spent on defense can be spent on more attackers/drones, whichever is convenient
[2018-07-05 19:29] swapgo: Walls and Forcefield and Aegises(Aegi?) Do nothing other than block, so buying them instead of buying something that actively generates value per turn is basically floating the gold
[2018-07-05 19:30] supergreyman (Greyman): When people say "Buy Rhinos when inflation is high", they're saying that buying Rhinos are good when there's a lot of attack pressure. Tarsiers are horrible when you need attack/defense *right now* instead of two turns later.
[2018-07-05 19:30] swapgo: Yeh, same goes for Immolite
[2018-07-05 19:31] swapgo: Immolite is a bad attacker, but it can attack instantly and, sometimes, in very desperate times, throw itself as a chump block
[2018-07-05 19:31] miuna6933 (Aka): low inflation = make long term investment
high inflation = do things now or die
[2018-07-05 19:32] supergreyman (Greyman): When I'm pressuring you with 12 attacks per turn while you're being forced to buy several walls/forcefields per turn (and not enough money to buy anything else), buying Rhinos is way more valuable than Tarsiers.
[2018-07-05 19:33] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, Aka got the simple definition down.
[2018-07-05 19:35] miuna6933 (Aka): grimbotch is amazing when you don't need it to last more than 4 turns, tarsier is better early because it lasts forever but there's a tempo loss since it's so slow
[2018-07-05 19:35] supergreyman (Greyman): When you're *about* to be pressured to buy only defense per turn, buying Rhinos ahead of time help you make use of 2 staminas and soak with it immediately afterward (aka Rhino train). If you time it too late, you might end up not being able to make full use of it.
[2018-07-05 19:36] miuna6933 (Aka): and if the game is decided in 1 more turn then immolite is even better
[2018-07-05 19:37] supergreyman (Greyman): Thus, to make full use of Rhinos ability, you need to  read several turns ahead, which is what SwapGo was referring to.
[2018-07-05 19:38] 1m2o: Rhino train nto best example i prefer grimbotch train sentinel train e.t.c ;D
[2018-07-05 19:39] supergreyman (Greyman): Chieftain train, Bombarder train....
[2018-07-05 19:40] swapgo: Not calling it "Chieftrain"
[2018-07-05 19:40] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): buy redeemer, attack with redeemer, soak with redeemer to defend the gauss charges :ThinkTank:
[2018-07-05 19:41] swapgo: Chieftrain is absurdly strong if well set-up
[2018-07-05 19:41] supergreyman (Greyman): That thing is too good.
[2018-07-05 19:41] swapgo: 7 Health is so much, the hard part is finding a window to start it
[2018-07-05 19:41] cachotognax: with immolites and elecrtrovores in the set
[2018-07-05 19:42] cachotognax: I couldn't keep it consistent since I dind't have the eco
[2018-07-05 19:42] cachotognax: and my opponent could just hold the immolites to find the holes.
[2018-07-05 19:42] swapgo: Right, Imolite makes it trickier
[2018-07-05 19:43] swapgo: I was bullying an Iso setup in a game by buying Doomed Mech before each shot, so their shots synced with my soak
[2018-07-05 19:44] supergreyman (Greyman): One time I bullied Iso player by buying Redeemer when they're at Exhaust 2. Annoyed the heck out of them. 😄
[2018-07-05 19:45] cachotognax: that's a good way to sneak a reedemer in
[2018-07-05 19:45] swapgo: Kronus is very bullyable but also frighteningly efficient when there's no counterplay
[2018-07-05 19:45] cachotognax: speaking of reddemer, you kinda want to absorb with walls since 3 attack for 1 absorb isn't that good a deal
[2018-07-05 19:46] swapgo: Absorbing on Deemer is last resort only
[2018-07-05 19:46] cachotognax: I'm especially talking about the turns the charges hit you back
[2018-07-05 19:46] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, you almost never want to block with it.
[2018-07-05 19:47] swapgo: I'd rather gambit for a small breach than absorb on Redeemer usually
[2018-07-05 19:53] supergreyman (Greyman): The thing for starting Chieftain train is to predict 3 turns ahead if you can use it to block 7+absorb value based on how fast your opponent is ramping damage. If he's going like +1 attacker per turn and your best absorber is a Wall, you can start when he's like around 6 damage.
[2018-07-05 19:54] supergreyman (Greyman): And somehow you need to be able to provide defense in the meantime....which is like the hardest part to me.

[2018-07-05 20:00] swapgo: It eats up one of your Bs three turns in advance
[2018-07-05 20:00] swapgo: And you probably have to buy a second conduit along the way
